description: 57 - 14-01-2025 - Meeting between Peter Mandelson and Pat McFadden (former Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster and Minister for Intergovernmental Relations)
text:
Duchy of Lancaster and Minister for Intergovernmental Relations)

Lord Mandelson readout

Hi all,

CDL met with the HM Ambassador-Designate to the United States, Lord Mandelson, on

Tuesday 14 January. Please find a readout below and cascade as appropriate.

Attendees:
e Lord Mandelson, HMA-Designate to the US
. Jes CDL’s Office

Actions:

e CDL referenced an article by Robert Kagan on Russia/Ukraine and asked that this
was shared with Lord Mandelson. I've linked it here and passed it on to Lord
Mandelson’s team.

CDL and Lord Mandelson discussed the implications for the change of administration for the
UK’s relationship with the US. Lord Mandelson noted that the Government should approach
the relationship with an intensely relaxed approach, rather than being concerned with optics
or gestures.

CDL updated on the PM Small Strategy Group on the US, including the Government's aims,
and that the position of the UK outside major trading blocks was both vulnerable and agile.
There were significant trade opportunities with China that would not cause national security
concerns. However, the UK’s relationship with China would be affected by the overarching
dynamics of the US-China relationship. The Government had room to manoeuvre its audit to
make a decision that was in the UK national interest, but the implementation was as
important as the strategy itself.

Trade negotiations with the US were discussed, where the incoming administration would
likely focus on agriculture, especially considering the support required from Congress and
the farming lobby. CDL reaffirmed his support for the UK farming sector, and his desire not to
expose them to unfair competition.

The Russia/Ukraine war was also discussed, touching on the implications of different
outcomes of the conflict on the geopolitical position of key partners.

[ends]

184
parsing_notes: Extract meeting date/time, list of attendees (name + role), agenda/discussion topics as bullet items, any action points (AP 1:, AP 2: patterns). Attendees yield ATTENDED edges with date. Topics/APs are content nodes linked to the meeting event.
